data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Context
Story: 4c60-5e2e — As a DSO plugin developer, I can check if a ticket exists via the CLI in sub-millisecond time.

Add ticket exists <id> subcommand: directory + CREATE-file presence check, no reducer invocation, sub-10ms SLO.

## What to Build
Write tests/scripts/test-ticket-exists-dispatcher.sh that fails because the dispatcher does not yet have an 'exists' case.

## Test Approach (Given/When/Then)
Given: A synthetic TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR with a real directory for ticket 'e1' containing a CREATE event file, and no directory for 'missing-id'. An archived ticket 'arch1' has a directory with a CREATE file (archived via TOMBSTONE event).
When: The dispatcher is called with 'ticket exists <id>'
Then:
- 'e1' exits 0 (ticket exists)
- 'missing-id' exits non-zero (ticket does not exist)
- 'arch1' exits 0 (archived tickets still exist)
- No args exits non-zero (usage error)
- Output does NOT contain "unknown subcommand" or "unrecognized" for 'exists'

## Test Isolation Pattern
Unlike TICKET_CMD-injectable scripts, ticket-exists.sh reads the filesystem directly via TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR. Use synthetic fixture directories, not TICKET_CMD mocks (same pattern as test-ticket-purge-bridge-dispatcher.sh).

Fixture helper:
```bash
make_tracker_fixture() {
    local dir=$(mktemp -d)
    _CLEANUP_DIRS+=("$dir")
    # e1: existing ticket
    mkdir -p "$dir/e1"
    echo '{}' > "$dir/e1/$(date +%s)-CREATE.json"
    # arch1: archived ticket (directory still exists)
    mkdir -p "$dir/arch1"
    echo '{}' > "$dir/arch1/$(date +%s)-CREATE.json"
    echo "$dir"
}
```

Call: TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR="$fixture" "$DISPATCHER" exists e1

## Files to Modify
- tests/scripts/test-ticket-exists-dispatcher.sh — CREATE (new)

## RED Marker Required
After writing this test file, update .test-index with:
  plugins/dso/scripts/ticket:...tests/scripts/test-ticket-exists-dispatcher.sh

And add RED marker to the entry for the test function:
  tests/scripts/test-ticket-exists-dispatcher.sh [test_exists_routes_through_dispatcher]

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-ticket-exists-dispatcher.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-ticket-exists-dispatcher.sh
- [ ] RED marker exists in .test-index for test_exists_routes_through_dispatcher
  Verify: grep '\[test_exists_routes_through_dispatcher\]' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] Test suite currently FAILS (dispatcher has no 'exists' case yet)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-ticket-exists-dispatcher.sh; test $? -ne 0
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (RED marker tolerates the failing test)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py passes
  Verify: ruff check $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/*.py 2>/dev/null || true

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
data.title: [RED] Write failing integration test for ticket exists dispatcher
